claims.subject: Microsoft

claims.subject: Microsoft

evidence.quote: Microsoft and AMD decided to announce that the two were teaming up for large scale AI systems based on the AMD “Helios” rack design and including a slew of hardware and software technologies from AMD, including the its “Altair” MI455X GPUs, its “Venice” Epyc 9006 CPUs, its Pensando DPUs, and the ROCm software stack.

Microsoft has committed to rolling out clusters of Venice Epyc CPUs that will underpin in two different instances on the Azure cloud. The Azure HDv2 instances will be aimed at agentic AI workloads and their data pipeline processing, while the HXv2 instances (presumably with more cores) will be aimed at electronic design automation for chips.
Microsoft is working with AMD to move its Azure Boost acceleration software to the Pensando DPUs.

fields.value: Forward Deployed Engineer blending software engineering, solution consulting, and hands-on product strategy

value: Existing business systems requiring pipelines, authentication, and data flows
Salesforce orgs with object structure, automation rules, and data flows
Real production data tested early, not sample sets
Data pipelines tested against real production data
Data quality gaps identified during discovery
Governance and approval steps mapped and unlikely to stall a release
Data governance rules applied to live production data
AI carried from working demo into a system a real business can depend on
Unknown risk turned into a well-laid plan
Measurable business outcome instead of just a working demo

evidence.quote: Salesforce Forward Deployed AI Engineers configure those pieces to match how the business actually operates, rather than forcing operations to bend around a generic setup.
In another 2026 survey of enterprise-level technology professionals, less than 14% of companies with pilot agent initiatives have rolled out their agents enterprise-wide.
The role blends three skill sets that rarely sit in one person: software engineering, solution consulting, and hands-on product strategy.
AI models rarely fail in isolation. They fail at the connective tissue, the pipelines, authentication, and data flows that link a model to the systems a business already runs on.
Data pipelines have been tested against real production data, not sample sets.
Governance and approval steps are mapped and unlikely to stall a release.
A Forward Deployed Engineer is one of the clearest answers to that gap, because the role exists specifically to carry AI from a working demo into a system that a real business can depend on.
